batches.evidence: commit 6988ac3. Backlog A1 (quality LINT E501 at scripts/check_skill_requirements.py:562, 116 chars, the only >88-char line in the production scope). Fix: split the f-string into two adjacent literals via implicit string concatenation; concatenated value byte-identical (behavior-neutral). Dispatched to OpenCode worker (opencode-worker-bridge, deepseek-v4-pro, standard profile); orchestrator re-verified the diff (3 lines, nothing else touched) and re-ran the suite. Verified: python3 -m pytest tests/ -q -> 79 passed; awk length>88 over scripts/ -> 0.

17 docs doc_path_missing findings are won't-fix, not deferred work: 3 are runtime output-artifact paths correctly documented in SKILL.md (the leaf cannot distinguish output-path refs from repo-file refs), and 14 are references inside immutable historical records (docs/dogfood/2026-06-10-sp5-dogfood-report.md, docs/plans/2026-06-10-sp5-*.md) that correctly describe repo-A. The broad '--source-prefix docs' swept in those immutable records; a future repo-B docs gate (out of scope this round) would adopt a repo-A-style living-docs scope omitting docs/dogfood and docs/plans.

vulture DELETE on _extract_skill_name (confidence 60 == threshold floor) is a false positive: the function is directly exercised by test_extract_skill_name_missing_name and test_extract_skill_name_unreadable_file. Not actioned.

quality FORMAT format_drift not actioned: repo-B declares no ruff/format standard (no pyproject/ruff.toml, only a .ruff_cache dir) and ruff is not installed on this interpreter; a wholesale reformat of a never-ruff-formatted file is not a safe single-signal mechanical fix.

OpenCode worker (H2) was dispatched on the default port 4096, which was already bound by a concurrent track's server (pid 1219430, cwd /home/jakub/projects/repo-audit-skills = Track G). The bridge attached to it; the delegated session was isolated (separate session id) and every write landed only in repo-B (verified via git diff). Per the zero-shared-writes rule the non-owned server process was left running; Track H's own wrapper process exited cleanly and stranded nothing.

16 deferred-structural findings (7 complexity in scripts/check_skill_requirements.py + 9 hotspot churn/coupling/author-concentration) carried to the SP9 backlog; per the SP8 Track-H mandate repo-B receives NO gate extension this round.
